name: Author Byline Visible
description: Verifies a visible author name in page content (not just schema). Visible authorship is a primary trust signal for E-E-A-T-driven AI citation.
method_detail: Scan visible content for author name pattern

criteria.pass: Author name visible in page content (not just schema)

fix_template: Add visible "By [Author Name]" near the title/date
sources: 96% of AI Overview citations from E-E-A-T sources. Visible authorship is a primary trust signal. Schema-only author (invisible to users) is weaker than visible byline.

name: Author Schema With Credentials/sameAs
description: Checks that the author's Person schema includes sameAs external profile links plus jobTitle or description. sameAs verification lets AI systems confirm expertise; a name-only Person schema is an unverifiable claim.
method_detail: Person schema has sameAs or jobTitle

criteria.pass: Person schema has `sameAs` array with 1+ external profile URLs AND (`jobTitle` OR `description`)
criteria.warn: Person schema exists but missing sameAs or credentials
criteria.fail: No Person schema for author, or Person with name only

sources: Author entity verification through sameAs links enables AI systems to confirm expertise. Empty Person schema (name only) is an unverifiable claim.

name: Outbound Citations to Primary Sources
description: Counts outbound links to authoritative external domains (.gov, .edu, academic, official documentation). Citing sources is the highest-impact GEO strategy because AI engines cross-reference claims.

criteria.pass: 3+ outbound links to authoritative external domains (.gov, .edu, academic journals, official documentation, recognized data sources)
criteria.fail: Zero outbound links to external authoritative sources

fix_template: Add inline citations: `According to [Source Name](https://...), [factual claim].`
sources: GEO paper: "Cite Sources" = 30-40% visibility boost (highest-impact strategy, universally effective). All AI engines cross-reference claims. Outbound citations enable corroboration.

name: Publication Date Visible AND in Schema
description: Verifies the publication date appears both as visible page text and as datePublished in schema. Visible date gives user transparency; schema date is machine-readable for AI systems — both together are strongest.
method_detail: Visible date on page + datePublished in schema

criteria.pass: Date visible on page (text like "Published: Jan 15, 2026") AND datePublished in schema
criteria.warn: Date in one location but not both

fix_template: Add visible date near title AND `"datePublished": "2026-01-15"` in schema
sources: Google API leak: three date signals. Visible date provides transparency to users. Schema date is machine-readable for AI systems. Both together are strongest.

name: dateModified Visible AND in Schema
description: Verifies a visible "Updated" indicator plus a schema dateModified distinct from datePublished. dateModified is the primary freshness signal for AI engines.

criteria.pass: "Updated" or "Last modified" visible on page AND dateModified in schema with date different from datePublished
criteria.warn: dateModified in schema only, not visible

fix_template: Add visible "Last updated: [date]" AND `"dateModified": "2026-04-10"` in schema. Update both when content changes.
sources: Perplexity: content within 30 days gets 3.2x more citations. 83% of commercial AI citations from pages < 12 months old. dateModified is THE freshness signal for AI engines.

name: Organization Schema With sameAs
description: Checks the Organization schema for a sameAs array of resolving social profile URLs. AI systems use sameAs for entity disambiguation; missing sameAs weakens entity recognition.

criteria.pass: Organization schema has `sameAs` array with 2+ social profile URLs that resolve
criteria.fail: Organization schema has no sameAs, or no Organization schema

sources: sameAs links establish entity identity across platforms. AI systems use sameAs for entity disambiguation. Missing sameAs = weaker entity recognition.

name: Privacy/Terms Accessible
description: Checks the footer for privacy/terms/legal links. A minor transparency signal aligned with quality rater guidelines; absence suggests a less trustworthy site.

criteria.pass: Footer contains links with text matching "privacy", "terms", "legal", or "cookie"

fix_template: Add footer links to privacy policy and terms of service pages
sources: Minor trust signal. Quality rater guidelines check for transparency indicators. Absence suggests less trustworthy site.

name: HTTPS Valid
description: Verifies the page loads over HTTPS without certificate errors. HTTPS is a confirmed ranking signal and AI crawlers deprioritize non-HTTPS sources.

criteria.pass: Page loads over HTTPS without certificate errors
criteria.fail: Certificate error, expired cert, or HTTP-only

fix_template: Install valid SSL certificate. Configure server for HTTPS.
sources: HTTPS is a confirmed ranking signal (since 2014). All major browsers warn users on HTTP sites. AI crawlers deprioritize non-HTTPS sources.

name: Content Freshness Recency
description: Grades content age from dateModified/datePublished and flags likely cosmetic timestamp updates. Content freshness is a primary signal for all AI answer engines; the age grading is deterministic while cosmetic-update detection is model judgment.
method_detail: dateModified age: < 90d pass, 90-365d warn, > 365d fail. Cosmetic update detection via LLM

criteria.pass: dateModified or datePublished is < 90 days old
criteria.fail: Date is > 365 days old; OR no datePublished AND no dateModified AND no visible date → FAIL "No freshness signal. AI engines cannot determine if this content is current." (overlaps with G4/G5 but specifically checks for recency assessment capability)
criteria.cosmetic_update_detection: If dateModified is recent (< 30 days) but content references outdated data (years, statistics, "in 2024" language, discontinued products), flag as MODEL JUDGMENT warning: "Possible cosmetic timestamp update without substantive content changes (Sieve AP#799)."

fix_template: If no date: Add `"dateModified": "YYYY-MM-DD"` to schema + visible "Last updated: [date]" on page → fix type: PAGE HTML FIX. If date is stale: Actually update the content — refresh statistics, update examples, add new information — THEN update dateModified → fix type: CONTENT RESTRUCTURE. Never just change the date without changing content (Sieve AP#799: "Cosmetic timestamp updates" — risk: medium)
sources: 50% of AI-cited content is < 13 weeks old (2026 web data). Perplexity: 3.2x citation boost for content < 30 days. 83% of commercial AI citations from pages < 12 months. Content freshness is a primary signal for ALL AI answer engines, not just a nice-to-have.
notes: Source assigns a dual badge (HARD EVIDENCE for date recency + MODEL JUDGMENT for cosmetic-update detection); collapsed to hard_evidence as primary with secondary_badge recording the model-judgment component. Fix type is conditional in the source: page_html when no date exists, content_restructure when the date is stale.
